# Data Architecture Implementation Plan
## Executive Summary
Based on Salesforce's proven platform capabilities and implementation methodologies, we recommend a structured approach to implementing a robust data architecture that leverages our low-code/no-code platform and industry best practices.
## Implementation Approach
### Phase 1: Assessment and Planning (Weeks 1-4)
**Data Needs Assessment**
Our implementation approach begins with a comprehensive data needs assessment utilizing proven methodologies. Drawing from our Tableau Blueprint Workshop framework, we conduct foundational discovery across five core assessment areas: Strategy and Teams, Governance, Agility, Proficiency, and Community. **Master Data Management Strategy**
The Salesforce data model is based on objects, which allows the application to be highly flexible. Users can create new record objects as well as designate both standard and custom fields within a configurable layout. Salesforce supports multiple options within Master Data Management (MDM) design patterns, with two common use cases: Salesforce working with a third-party MDM solution or Salesforce being the MDM platform.
### Phase 2: Foundation Setup (Weeks 5-12)
**Platform Configuration**
The Salesforce platform supports a "low code - no code" concept, which allows your organization to "configure" rather than "code" the desired solution. The platform allows you to rapidly configure and deploy new features or new applications often in weeks, not years. Developers and business users can use the platform's simple yet powerful declarative, point-and-click tools to perform their core tasks at lightning speed.
**Data Quality Controls**
Salesforce offers many features to ensure the capture of effective and relevant customer data, including:
- Validation rules with red-highlighted error messages
- Administrator-defined field pick lists
- Field default values and required fields
- Bubble help text on data entry screens
- Field history tracking that records all field changes with user, date, and changed value for selected tracking fields
### Phase 3: Implementation and Integration (Weeks 13-24)
**MDM Implementation Best Practices**
One key best practice in implementing MDM is to minimize the number of sources or systems of entry. Channeling all entity creation through Salesforce can help ensure that duplicates are minimized and that there are fewer conflicting versions to be merged. Field controls can be added to Salesforce to enforce a search of the MDM system before creating new master records.
**API-First Integration**
Driven by the API-first principle, apps can use open APIs based on industry standards such as REST and SOAP to add data from other systems. For more complex apps, developers can leverage the Apex programming language, which is an object-oriented, on-demand language similar to Java with strongly-typed, compiled on demand functionality.

## Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity Planning Operations
Salesforce maintains a comprehensive disaster recovery and business continuity framework designed to ensure service resilience and data protection across our platform ecosystem.
### Disaster Recovery Infrastructure and Architecture
Salesforce employs a robust disaster recovery strategy built on geographically distributed infrastructure. Each Salesforce instance operates from two geographically separate locations, with an active site serving production traffic while transactions replicate in near real-time to a completely redundant ready site. This dual-site architecture enables site switching for maintenance, compliance, and disaster recovery purposes.
Customer data, including all committed transactions, is replicated to disk in near real-time at the designated disaster recovery data center. Daily backups are performed at each data center facility without interrupting application access, with backup cloning transmitted over encrypted MPLS networks across all data centers. Backups are retained for 90 days and never physically leave secure data center facilities except for secure destruction processes.
### Recovery Objectives and Service Level Commitments
Salesforce disaster recovery plans establish specific recovery objectives:
- **Recovery Time Objective (RTO)**: 12 hours for restoration of covered services after disaster declaration
- **Recovery Point Objective (RPO)**: Maximum customer data loss of 4 hours
These targets apply to production environments but exclude scenarios involving simultaneous compromise of both data centers and do not cover development or test environments such as Sandbox services.
### Platform-Specific Capabilities
**Hyperforce Architecture**: Salesforce Government Cloud Plus service operates with 100% capacity replication between primary and secondary data centers. The secondary site is geographically separated within separate AWS GovCloud Availability Zones, with data transmission occurring over encrypted links. Continuous site switching programs verify projected recovery times and validate data replication effectiveness.
**Tableau Cloud**: Built on high-availability architecture across network, compute, and storage layers hosted in AWS. The platform utilizes an Active-Active configuration for application tiers and active-passive configuration for data tiers to ensure seamless failovers. A disaster recovery cluster operates in a separate availability zone to handle catastrophic failures.
**MuleSoft**: Maintains documented disaster recovery plans reviewed and tested annually by Security and Service Engineering teams. Customer data is stored redundantly across multiple hosting provider data center locations with automated nightly backups and 90-day testing cycles to validate restoration procedures.
### Continuous Validation and Testing
Salesforce implements a Continuous Site Switching program, performing active and ready instance location switches approximately every six months. This program enables continuous operational improvements while satisfying internal compliance requirements for customers requiring geographic distribution capabilities throughout the year.
The disaster recovery plan undergoes constant measurement against regulatory and governance requirements and serves as a crucial component of production environment change acceptance processes. Annual disaster recovery activities include failover testing to alternate processing sites for selected instances to validate established RTO and RPO targets.
### Incident Response Integration
Salesforce maintains an established Security Incident Response process integrated with disaster recovery operations. The company commits to prompt customer notification in the event of actual or reasonably suspected unauthorized data disclosure, with notification methods including phone contact, email to administrators and security contacts, and public posting on trust.salesforce.com.
This comprehensive approach ensures business continuity across the Salesforce ecosystem while maintaining the security and compliance standards required for government and enterprise operations.

## Data Retention and Lifecycle Management
Our data retention methodology follows a structured approach: Active customer data remains in storage until the customer deletes or changes it. Data deleted by customers is temporarily available in the application Recycle Bin for 15 days, after which records are marked for deletion. Data marked for deletion is permanently deleted by batch jobs over a 90-day period, with both customer data and data marked for deletion retained on backup media for 90 days (30 days for sandbox instances).
## Access Control and Security Framework
Application access to all data is controlled through comprehensive security features and access controls that apply to both users and integrations. The platform implements four sharing models: Private, Public Read Only, Public Read/Write, and Public Read/Write/Transfer. Organizations can specify object access through permission sets and profiles, field access through field-level security, and individual record access through organization-wide sharing settings, role hierarchies, and sharing rules.
## Compliance and Standards Adherence
Our methodology aligns with industry-accepted best practices and government security requirements. The platform adheres to DISA Security Technical Implementation Guides (STIGs) and Center for Internet Security (CIS) Benchmarks where available. All changes undergo approval, testing, and security impact analysis before deployment, with significant changes requiring thorough impact assessments as defined by FedRAMP policies.
This methodology ensures that data integration, conversion, and migration activities maintain the highest standards of security, compliance, and operational reliability while providing the flexibility needed for diverse enterprise requirements.

Salesforce allows the creation and management of roles and profiles to control user access and permissions. Profiles define the baseline permissions for what users can do within Salesforce, such as access to objects, fields, and specific functionalities. Roles, on the other hand, establish a hierarchy that determines data visibility and access based on the user's position within the organization (Source: Knowledge Articles).
## Multi-Layered Access Management
The platform provides a flexible, layered sharing design that lets an organization expose specific application components and data sets to different sets of users through:
- **Organization-wide sharing**: Default settings that provide a baseline level of access for each object. - **Permission Sets**: These are used to grant additional permissions to users without changing their profiles. This is useful for temporary access or for granting permissions that are not covered by the user's profile (Source: Knowledge Articles).
- **Field-Level Security**: Control access to individual fields within an object. This ensures that sensitive information is only accessible to users who need it (Source: Knowledge Articles).
- **Object-Level Security**: Define access to entire objects, ensuring that users can only interact with objects that are relevant to their role (Source: Knowledge Articles).

### Role Hierarchy Management
Roles are used to create a hierarchy that mirrors the organizational structure. This hierarchy controls data access, allowing users to access data owned or shared by their subordinates. Role assignment ensures users have the appropriate level of access, which is particularly important for managers who need to oversee the work of their team members (Source: Knowledge Articles).
### Advanced Access Controls
The platform supports fine-grained, row-level security that can be implemented by synchronizing a user's login account with an "Entitlement" table. This allows administrators to manage a reduced number of secure workbooks, such as maintaining a single dashboard for all regions, clients, or teams, with row-level security automatically applied. Users will only see their own data (Source: Knowledge Articles).

The Salesforce Government Cloud Plus maintains strict segregation of duties and least privilege, ensuring that employees are granted only the necessary level of access to the production network to perform their assigned job functions based on role. This is aligned with compliance controls AC-5 and AC-6 (Source: Security Documentation).
The platform also enforces two-factor authentication processes to verify the authentication of access requests to internal systems, with authentication being NIST SP 800-63B AAL3 compliant utilizing FIPS 140 compliant authenticators (Source: Security Documentation).
This comprehensive role-based access control framework enables organizations to effectively manage access privileges for all user types including job seekers, workforce program participants, employers, providers, staff, and system administrators while maintaining appropriate security controls and compliance requirements.

Our proposed solution is built on the Salesforce Platform and delivered as a Software as a Service (SaaS) offering with minimal client-side requirements. The solution operates through standard web browsers with no software installation or hardware procurement required on your end. Users need only a computer capable of running a web browser and an internet connection, or a mobile device for access. This eliminates the need for your organization to manage software licensing or hardware platforms.
**Platform Infrastructure**
The Salesforce Platform operates on a metadata-driven software architecture specifically designed for cloud computing and multitenancy. Our infrastructure combines several persistence technologies, including a custom-designed relational database schema inherently built for cloud environments without requiring virtualization. The platform utilizes a runtime engine that materializes all application data from metadata, ensuring clear separation between the compiled runtime database engine, tenant data, and application metadata.
**Server Hardening and Operating System**
Our web and application servers are Linux-based systems that undergo comprehensive hardening procedures. All new systems are deployed from known baseline images, with unused services, ports, and packages deactivated. We maintain minimal user accounts with no shared password files, and all configurations conform to industry-accepted Center for Internet Security (CIS) system hardening guidelines that address known security issues.
**Scalability and Performance Architecture**
The platform architecture supports scalability to accommodate billions of transactions daily through its multitenant design. Our metadata-driven approach uses sophisticated metadata caches to maintain frequently used metadata in memory, avoiding performance-degrading disk I/O operations and code recompilations while improving application response times. The modular instance architecture allows each instance to support several thousand customers securely and efficiently.
**Disaster Recovery Specifications**
Our disaster recovery plans maintain specific recovery objectives: restoration of covered services within 12 hours after disaster declaration (Recovery Time Objective) and maximum customer data loss of 4 hours (Recovery Point Objective). These targets apply to production environments and exclude scenarios where both data centers are simultaneously compromised.
**Integration and Extensibility Requirements**
The platform provides open APIs that enable seamless integration with legacy applications, third-party solutions, or external cloud-based services. Native integration exists between Salesforce Platform SaaS products without requiring special integration to connect or sync data. The solution supports multiple seasonal release updates annually included in the subscription service without re-configuration, customization, or additional costs.
**Client-Side Considerations**
For organizations utilizing Tableau components within the broader Salesforce ecosystem, Tableau Online operates as a fully-hosted SaaS solution requiring only ancillary software such as Tableau Desktop and Tableau Bridge for installation. Performance and security statistics are available in real-time online, including details of planned downtime.
**Compliance and Security Framework**
The solution maintains comprehensive security measures including FedRAMP authorization capabilities. Our formal production deployment process includes build checklists, server hardening checklists, and pre-production testing with management approval required before infrastructure goes live.
This architecture ensures your organization receives a scalable, secure, and maintainable platform solution with minimal infrastructure requirements on your end while maintaining enterprise-grade performance and compliance capabilities.

Question: Describe the security system used by your proposed Solution, including: Authentication, Session Management, Access Control, Malicious Input Handling, Error Handling and Logging, System Monitoring
AI Generated Answer:
## Security System Overview
Salesforce implements a comprehensive, multi-layered security architecture that addresses all the requested security domains through our Government Cloud Plus and Hyperforce platforms.
### Authentication
Salesforce Government Cloud Plus provides robust authentication mechanisms that exceed industry standards. The platform requires users to provide username and password credentials by default, with mandatory multi-factor authentication (MFA) requirements for enhanced protection against phishing, credential stuffing, and account takeover attacks.
For government customers requiring enhanced authentication, we support federated authentication using Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) and delegated authentication that integrates with Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) services. Specifically, customers requiring Government-issued smart cards can implement authentication via Common Access Card (CAC) or Personal Identity Verification (PIV) cards through our federated authentication capability. Salesforce also offers native certificate-based authentication for CAC and PIV-based user authentication.
All passwords are stored using SHA 256 one-way hash format, ensuring credential security even in the unlikely event of unauthorized access to authentication databases.
### Session Management
Session security controls protect established user sessions through configurable timeout periods that terminate idle sessions, defending against unauthorized access when users leave computers unattended without properly disconnecting. When users request connections for the first time using new computer-browser-IP address combinations, Salesforce notices these requests, sends verification emails, and requires users to confirm their identity by clicking activation links.
### Access Control
Salesforce implements a flexible, layered sharing design that enables organizations to expose specific application components and data sets to different user groups. The platform enforces segregation of duties and least privilege principles, ensuring employees receive only the necessary access levels to perform their assigned job functions based on role.
Access control includes:
- Organization-wide sharing with default baseline access levels for each object
- Role hierarchy-based data access through sharing models
- Administrator-assigned data security rules determining user data access
- Network-based security features limiting connection times and locations through IP address restrictions
### Malicious Input Handling and System Monitoring
Salesforce employs sophisticated security tools that monitor platform activity in real-time to detect malicious events, threats, and intrusion attempts. Our Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) use machine learning and anomaly detection to identify and prioritize potential threats, monitoring network activity, data access patterns, and account behavior within the processing environment.
Network security measures include:
- External firewalls allowing only HTTP and HTTPS traffic on ports 80 and 443, plus ICMP traffic
- IDS sensors protecting all network segments
- RFC 1918 standard network compliance through switches
- Address translation technologies for enhanced security
- Two-factor authentication protection for internal software systems
The platform integrates with multiple threat intelligence feeds and receives threat alerts from sources including SANS and the United States Computer Emergency Readiness Team. Applications are continually monitored for security violation attempts.
### Error Handling and Logging
Salesforce maintains comprehensive logging capabilities that capture system activity. Infrastructure and AWS logging forward logs to a central logging system residing within the authorization boundary. Activity logs from production devices and servers are sent to logging and alerting systems that report and alert on events in real-time.
Host activity is logged to remote, central locations for safekeeping, ensuring complete audit trails for security analysis and compliance requirements.
### Continuous Monitoring and Compliance
The Salesforce application and website receive 24x7 monitoring for reliability and performance through our Site Reliability team and Subject Matter Experts. Our Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) provides comprehensive monitoring of the production environment alongside automated monitoring tools that route issues and alerts to on-call personnel and engineering teams.
### Third-Party Validation
Our security controls undergo rigorous third-party validation including ISO 27001 certification, SysTrust audits, and annual SOC 1, SOC 2, and SOC 3 audits. For government customers, FedRAMP and DoD audits are conducted annually, with our current authorizations listed at the FedRAMP marketplace.
This multi-layered security approach ensures that customer data confidentiality, integrity, and availability remain protected through industry-leading security practices and technologies that have been validated by independent auditors and government assessment organizations.

## System Redundancy and Failure Safety
Based on the comprehensive documentation provided, Salesforce implements extensive redundancy measures across all central components to ensure continuous operations in the event of individual component failures.
### Infrastructure Redundancy Architecture
**Network-Level Redundancy:** Salesforce data centers maintain no single points of failure in network architecture. The infrastructure includes multiple network carriers for customer connectivity, multiple ISPs for customer transit and internal replication, and redundant cloud-based networking infrastructure. Data centers are strategically located at major internet hubs with access to diverse network providers, including peering at Equinix Exchange with more than 20 peer networks available across dedicated hubs in the US, APAC, and Europe.
**Server and Application Redundancy:** The Salesforce service is built for high availability across three availability zones within each region. Web, Application, API, Cache, Search, Index, Query, and Batch servers are load balanced and distributed within multiple availability zones with failover capabilities. The platform implements N+1 redundancy at minimum, where N represents the number of components required for service operation, and +1 provides the redundancy buffer. In many cases, Salesforce maintains more than one redundant infrastructure component for each function.
### Database Redundancy and Data Protection
**Multi-Zone Database Replication:** Database servers are replicated both within the same availability zone for performance optimization and across three distinct availability zones to ensure availability. This enterprise-class recoverability design provides comprehensive data protection against localized failures.
**Continuous Backup and Recovery:** For Salesforce Government Cloud Plus specifically, the service maintains 100% capacity replication between primary and secondary data centers. The secondary site is geographically separated within distinct AWS Availability Zones, with data transmitted across encrypted links. Continuous site switching programs verify projected recovery times and validate data replication integrity between data centers.
### Automated Failover Capabilities
**Network Failover:** The routing policy is structured so that traffic automatically switches from primary ISP to backup ISP via BGP when the primary becomes unavailable. This automated failover ensures network continuity without manual intervention.
**System Monitoring and Response:** The platform includes 24/7 monitoring with automated tools that route issues, warnings, and problems to Site Reliability teams. Extensive monitoring and instrumentation are built into the application itself, enabling accurate health and performance reporting to support staff and engineers.
### Compliance and Standards Alignment
**Regulatory Compliance:** These redundancy measures support compliance with multiple frameworks including FedRAMP High, DoD IL4, SOC 1, SOC 2, SOC 3, and ISO 27001. The architecture is designed to eliminate single points of failure while maintaining application performance and meeting government security requirements.
**Carrier-Neutral Strategy:** The implementation of a carrier-neutral network strategy minimizes risks associated with dependency on single providers while providing a highly resilient environment with maximum uptime and performance.
This comprehensive redundancy architecture ensures that individual component failures do not result in complete operational interruption, meeting the requirement for failure safety through systematic redundancy of central components.

**Platform Foundation and Approach**
Salesforce delivers a comprehensive Software as a Service (SaaS) solution built on a metadata-driven architecture that enables rapid implementation without traditional infrastructure requirements. The platform requires only a computer with web browser capability and internet connection, eliminating the need for hardware procurement, software licensing, or infrastructure management by your organization.

This methodology enables rapid deployment of new features and applications, often within weeks rather than years. This approach establishes continuous feedback loops between program leaders, stakeholders, and IT teams, enabling requirements adjustments before significant resources are expended. The Agile methodology proves more cost-effective than traditional waterfall approaches by reducing over-budget projects and ensuring solutions meet organizational expectations.
**Master Data Management Architecture**
Salesforce supports multiple Master Data Management (MDM) design patterns, including integration with third-party MDM solutions or serving as the core MDM platform. The platform's object-based data model provides flexibility for creating custom record objects and fields within configurable layouts. Validation rules, workflow logic, and pre-integrated applications extend system capabilities through point-and-click interfaces.

The Salesforce platform utilizes a runtime engine that materializes application data from metadata, creating clear separation between the compiled runtime database engine, tenant data, and application-describing metadata. This architecture enables independent updates to system kernels and tenant-specific applications with minimal risk of interference.
The platform manages all logical database objects through metadata stored in the Universal Data Dictionary (UDD). Objects, fields, stored procedures, and database triggers exist as metadata rather than physical database constructs, enabling rapid schema modifications through simple metadata updates without blocking operations.

Salesforce implements encryption at multiple levels. For organizations requiring advanced data-at-rest encryption with customer key control, Salesforce offers Shield Platform Encryption as an additional licensing option. This solution provides customers the ability to provide their own keys along with comprehensive encryption key lifecycle management features.
Shield Platform Encryption utilizes Hardware Security Module (HSM) based key management infrastructure. The HSM and Digital Encryption Keys (DEK) are derived in real-time and never persist in either the database or file system. Customers can use open source crypto libraries, their existing HSM infrastructure, or third-party key brokering services to create and manage tenant secrets outside Salesforce.

The default Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) service combines customer-uploaded key material with Salesforce's master secret to derive a unique data encryption key. ## Additional Integration Options
For organizations requiring additional encryption capabilities, Salesforce supports integration with third-party Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) solutions that offer data loss prevention, user activity monitoring, malware protection, and data protection with encryption and tokenization.
**Assessment**: Salesforce provides strong encryption capabilities with unique keys. ### Core Technology Architecture
Salesforce Platform's core technology uses a runtime engine that materializes all application data from metadata?data about the data itself. In Salesforce Platform's well-defined metadata-driven architecture, there is a clear separation of the compiled runtime database engine (kernel), tenant data, and the metadata that describes each application. Question: Describe the security system used by your proposed Solution, including: Authentication, Session Management, Access Control, Malicious Input Handling, Error Handling and Logging, System Monitoring, Cybersecurity and Data Protection, Communications, Mobile Application.
AI Generated Answer:
# Security System Description for Salesforce Solutions
Salesforce employs a comprehensive, multi-layered security approach that addresses all components of your security requirements. Our security implementation spans application-level protections, infrastructure safeguards, and operational monitoring capabilities.
## Authentication and Access Control
Salesforce implements robust identity and access management capabilities through multiple mechanisms. The platform supports federated authentication using Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) and delegated authentication options, enabling integration with existing organizational authentication systems including Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) services.
For government customers requiring Common Access Card (CAC) or Personal Identity Verification (PIV) authentication, Salesforce offers both federated authentication capabilities and native certificate-based authentication. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is strictly enforced, with customer-defined authentication policies governing all access. All passwords are stored using SHA 256 one-way hash format.
The platform employs a flexible, layered sharing design that enables organizations to expose specific application components and data sets to different user groups. Organization-wide sharing defaults provide baseline access levels, while role hierarchies and sharing rules enable granular data access control. Administrators can assign data security rules that precisely determine which data users can access based on their roles and responsibilities.
## Session Management and Network Security
Network-based security features restrict access based on time and location parameters. IP address restrictions deny connections from unknown addresses, protecting against unauthorized access and phishing attacks. When users attempt connections from new computer-browser-IP address combinations, the system requires email-based identity confirmation.
Session security includes configurable idle session termination to protect against unauthorized access when users leave workstations unattended. External firewalls restrict traffic to HTTP and HTTPS on ports 80 and 443, plus ICMP traffic, preventing malicious attacks through unmonitored ports.
## Malicious Input Handling and Application Security
Salesforce implements comprehensive protections against common web application vulnerabilities. User-submitted data is never inserted into SQL strings directly - all code is properly escaped to prevent cross-site scripting attacks, and submitted parameters are bound to SQL using bind variables.
The platform utilizes SOQL (Salesforce Object Query Language) instead of traditional SQL, which significantly reduces the attack surface. SOQL permits only SELECT statements - users cannot execute INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE operations. This restriction eliminates command execution capabilities, preventing exploitation of the underlying operating system and blocking destructive interactions or unauthorized data manipulation.
## System Monitoring and Intrusion Detection
Comprehensive 24x7 monitoring encompasses both operational and security dimensions. The Site Reliability team provides continuous service monitoring with subject matter experts handling first and second-tier support, while technical engineers provide escalation capabilities.
Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) monitor the production network for potentially malicious traffic using machine learning and anomaly detection to identify and prioritize potential threats. These systems monitor network activity, data access patterns, and account behavior within the processing environment, integrated with multiple threat intelligence feeds.
Security monitoring includes activity logs from production devices and servers sent to centralized logging and alerting systems within the authorization boundary. The Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) provides comprehensive monitoring using various tools and third-party resources, with threat alerts received from sources including SANS and the United States Computer Emergency Readiness Team.
## Error Handling and Logging
Infrastructure and AWS logging captures system activity, with logs forwarded to central logging systems residing within the authorization boundary. Host activity is logged in remote, central locations for safekeeping, ensuring comprehensive audit trails.
Segregation of duties and least privilege principles ensure employees receive only necessary access levels to perform assigned job functions. All access and configuration changes undergo documented approval, testing, and security impact analysis before deployment.
## Cybersecurity and Data Protection
All customer data is encrypted by default at the volume level and in transit using TLS encryption. Device configurations are reviewed against industry best practices including DISA Security Technical Implementation Guides (STIGs) and Center for Internet Security (CIS) Benchmarks.
Media storage devices containing customer data are classified as Critical throughout their lifecycles. When storage devices reach end-of-life, decommissioning follows NIST SP 800-88 techniques, ensuring media containing customer data is not removed from control until securely decommissioned.
## Compliance and Third-Party Validation
Salesforce maintains extensive third-party certifications including ISO 27001, SOC 1, SOC 2, and SOC 3 audits performed annually at minimum, with FedRAMP and DoD audits conducted annually. Independent audits confirm security implementations exceed what most organizations achieve independently.
## Mobile Application Security
Salesforce provides comprehensive mobile applications for iOS and Android platforms offering secure access to platform capabilities. The mobile applications support offline access to workbooks and views, real-time updates with Tableau Pulse metrics, and robust security features including Single Sign-On (SSO) support and OAuth connections.
Mobile applications maintain the same security posture as web-based access, ensuring consistent authentication requirements and data protection across all access methods.
---
*Sources: Knowledge Articles (general security posture), HECVAT Assessment Data (compliance confirmations), Security Documentation (detailed implementation specifications)*

Question: Describe your approach to disaster recovery and Business Continuity Planning operations.
AI Generated Answer:
## Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity Planning Operations
Salesforce maintains a comprehensive disaster recovery and business continuity strategy built on industry-accepted methodologies and defense-in-depth principles. Our approach encompasses multiple layers of protection across our technology stack and operational processes.
### Disaster Recovery Infrastructure
Salesforce implements a robust dual-data center architecture where each instance is built and maintained in two geographically separate locations. The active site serves production traffic while transactions replicate in near real-time to a completely redundant ready site. This infrastructure model enables site switches for maintenance, compliance, and disaster recovery purposes without service interruption.
Customer data replication occurs up to the last committed transaction in near-real time at the designated disaster recovery data center. Additionally, data is backed up at the active data center and cloned at the ready site. Daily backups are performed at each facility without stopping application access, with backup cloning transmitted over encrypted MPLS networks across all data centers. Backups are retained for 90 days and never physically leave our secure facilities except through secure destruction processes.
### Recovery Objectives and Testing
Our disaster recovery plans maintain specific target recovery objectives:
- **Recovery Time Objective (RTO):** 12 hours for service restoration after disaster declaration
- **Recovery Point Objective (RPO):** Maximum 4-hour data loss window
These targets exclude scenarios involving simultaneous compromise of both data centers and exclude development environments such as Sandbox services.
As part of our Continuous Site Switching program, Salesforce switches active and ready instance locations approximately every six months. This practice continuously improves our operations and infrastructure while satisfying internal compliance requirements for customers whose instances must be served from either geographic location throughout the year.
### Platform-Specific Implementations
**Salesforce Government Cloud Plus** operates with 100% capacity replication between primary and secondary data centers, with geographic separation achieved through different Availability Zones within AWS GovCloud regions. Data transmission between sites occurs across encrypted links, and our continuous site switching program verifies projected recovery times and data replication integrity.
**Tableau Cloud** utilizes high-availability architecture across network, compute, and storage layers on AWS infrastructure. The platform employs Active-Active configuration for application tiers and active-passive configuration for data tiers to ensure seamless failovers. A dedicated disaster recovery cluster operates in a separate availability zone to handle catastrophic failures.
### Business Continuity Management
Salesforce maintains dedicated disaster recovery teams and documented plans that undergo annual review and testing by Security and Service Engineering personnel. Our Security Incident Response Team monitors for security incidents and follows established incident response procedures for prompt customer notification of any unauthorized data disclosure.
For Tableau products specifically, disaster recovery strategies and plans are reviewed and tested at least annually with updates as necessary. Database backups follow defined schedules according to established procedures, and annual disaster recovery activities include failover testing to alternate processing sites for selected instances.
Additional detailed information regarding Salesforce's disaster recovery capabilities can be provided through execution of a Non-Disclosure Agreement between Salesforce and your organization.
*Sources: Knowledge Articles (disaster recovery procedures and RTO/RPO specifications), Security Documentation (Government Cloud whitepaper and HECVAT assessments), HECVAT Assessment Data (compliance confirmations)*

- **Delegated Authentication**: Allows integration with customer-chosen authentication methods, including LDAP servers and token-based authentication systems. This method supports granular permission-level management, enabling selective user authentication approaches within the same organization.

## Data Retention and Deletion Practices
The platform implements structured data lifecycle management processes. Active customer data remains in storage until modified or deleted by the customer. Customer-deleted data remains temporarily accessible through the application Recycle Bin for 15 days, after which records are marked for deletion and become unavailable to users. Data marked for deletion undergoes permanent deletion through batch processes over a 90-day period. - **Trust and Compliance Documentation**: Accessible at https://trust.salesforce.com/en/trust-and-compliance-documentation/
- **Salesforce Infrastructure and Sub-processors documentation**: For data hosting and location specifics
## Advanced Security Architecture with Hyperforce
Our **Hyperforce infrastructure** represents the next-generation Salesforce architecture built for the public cloud, delivering enhanced capabilities specifically relevant to government requirements:
**Data Residency and Control**: Hyperforce provides customers choice and control over data residency while serving employees and customers globally, supporting compliance requirements.
**Security by Design**: The platform implements least-privileged control, zero-trust principles, and encryption of customer data at-rest and in-transit by default.
**Enhanced Agility**: Zero downtime updates and faster development environments, along with tremendous interoperability with AWS.
## Specialized Government Solutions
**Salesforce Government Cloud Plus** offers additional capabilities for public sector requirements:
- **IRS 1075 Compliance**: A Third-Party Assessment Organization (3PAO) has assessed and issued attestation that our environment meets requirements for handling Federal Tax Information (FTI)
- **NIST SP 800-171 Attestation**: Based on FedRAMP High and DoD Impact Level 4 (IL4) controls implementation
- **Department of Defense Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC)**: NIST SP 800-171 serves as the foundation for CMMC Level 2
## Security Governance and Controls Implementation
Our security approach follows the **ISO 27001/27002 framework** and aligns with **NIST SP 800-53** requirements, including:
- **Dedicated Security Teams**: Security Engineering and Security Product and Program Management divisions
- **Regular Assessments**: Both internal vulnerability assessments and external third-party audits
- **Comprehensive Policies**: Including Security Incident Response Plans, Vulnerability Management and Response Plans, and Disaster Recovery Plans
## Alternative Approaches and Considerations
**Shared Responsibility Model**: Salesforce operates under a partnership model that includes platform-level security while enabling customers to implement application-specific security features such as multi-factor authentication, principle of least privilege, and activity monitoring.
**Flexible Contracting Options**: Multiple procurement pathways are available to accommodate various government contracting requirements, including direct agreements, authorized reseller arrangements, and framework-based procurement options.
**Identity and Access Management**: Our External Identity product provides OpenID Connect and SAML compliance with 2FA and MFA capabilities, enabling authentication to cloud and on-premise applications while supporting up to 100 bespoke Experience Cloud sites.
## Technical Capabilities for Government Operations
The platform supports processing of **protected health information (PHI)** under HIPAA, **credit card information**, and provides **AI features** with planned enhancements over the next 12 months. These capabilities support diverse government use cases while maintaining compliance standards.
## Recommendation
We recommend scheduling a detailed technical briefing to review the complete Control Implementation Summary (CIS) document, which provides comprehensive mapping of Salesforce's control implementations. This document is available through your Salesforce representative and offers the detailed technical roadmap information requested for your evaluation.
The combination of our proven government track record, comprehensive compliance posture, and flexible implementation options positions Salesforce as a strategic partner capable of supporting the Department's digital transformation objectives while maintaining the highest standards of security and compliance.

When storage devices reach the end of their useful life, AWS decommissions media using techniques detailed in NIST SP 800-88, ensuring that media containing customer data remains under AWS control until it has been securely decommissioned (Security Documentation).
**Cryptographic Standards Compliance**
Salesforce produces, controls, and distributes asymmetric cryptographic keys in accordance with FIPS 140-2 standards. Platform Encryption utilizes a FIPS 140-2 validated module (Certificate # 3254) for customer data encryption within the Salesforce application (Knowledge Articles). **Unique Encryption Keys**
The encryption keys used as part of the Platform Encryption solution are unique to each customer organization, ensuring data segregation and security isolation across the multi-tenant environment (Knowledge Articles).
This comprehensive encryption approach ensures that your organization's data receives appropriate protection during storage operations and when stored on physical media, meeting the requirements for offline storage and transfer medium encryption through industry-standard cryptographic implementations.

Our network protection includes Transport Layer Security (TLS) cryptographic protocols with preference for TLS 1.2, and HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) enabled by default on all Salesforce pages. Network gateways and firewalls at external boundaries are configured to deny all traffic by default and allow by exception, with comprehensive traffic filtering and rate limiting capabilities.
AWS Security Groups function as virtual firewalls controlling communication boundaries and preventing unauthorized inter-service traffic. Stateful packet inspection firewalls examine all network packets to prevent unauthorized connections. Our infrastructure employs secure routing and traffic flow policies ensuring customer traffic remains encrypted until reaching FIPS 140-compliant load balancers within the Salesforce Government Cloud Plus authorization boundary.
## Vulnerability Management and Patching
MuleSoft remediates vulnerabilities based on criticality and priority through our standard patching process. The information security department prioritizes and evaluates vulnerabilities using a ticketing system to track remediation. This vulnerability management program undergoes annual review by SOC 2 assessors to ensure efficacy.
Our technical operations and security personnel continuously monitor vulnerability alerts and patch release notifications from vendors and security sources. When patches are released, senior technical and management personnel evaluate risk, severity, and mitigation efforts to prescribe appropriate action. Critical vulnerabilities receive ongoing assessment with patch prioritization based on vulnerability criticality.
## Change Management and System Maintenance
Patching follows established Change Management policies and procedures to track, test, and install updates with appropriate internal notifications. Depending on severity and system risk, security patches may be scheduled for immediate deployment or deferred to planned maintenance intervals. All approved patches are incorporated into system build images to maintain consistency.
## Physical and Environmental Controls
Salesforce inherits comprehensive physical and environmental controls from AWS GovCloud infrastructure, which maintains a FedRAMP JAB P-ATO at High impact level. Data centers utilize 24/7 global Security Operations Centers for monitoring and incident response, with professional security staff controlling physical access through multi-factor authentication mechanisms and CCTV surveillance systems.
## Monitoring and Detection Systems
Our security monitoring includes Intrusion Detection Systems monitoring production networks for malicious traffic, comprehensive logging and alerting systems capturing activity from production devices, and threat monitoring through our Computer Security Incident Response Team receiving alerts from sources including SANS, US-CERT, and OWASP. Vulnerability scans are performed at least monthly across all operating systems, databases, and applications.
## Site Redundancy and Protection
Salesforce Government Cloud Plus operates with 100% capacity replication between geographically separated primary and secondary data centers within different AWS GovCloud Availability Zones. Data transmission between sites utilizes encrypted links, with continuous site switching programs verifying recovery times and data replication integrity.
This comprehensive security framework demonstrates Salesforce's adherence to industry best practices for protecting customer data across all infrastructure components and maintains current security postures through systematic update and monitoring processes.

## Encryption Capabilities and AES 256-Bit Compliance
Salesforce provides encryption capabilities that meet and exceed the minimum AES 256-bit encryption requirement through multiple security layers and options.
**Data at Rest Encryption**
All customer data at rest is encrypted by default at the volume level using AWS Elastic Block Storage (EBS) with AES 256-bit encryption, as documented in our Government Cloud Plus security documentation. Additionally, information stored within the Multi-Tenant Database and Document Storage infrastructure is encrypted at rest within the file system, with encryption keys managed by Salesforce.
**Salesforce Classic Encryption**
The Classic Encryption feature automatically creates a unique customer encryption key for the exclusive use of each customer organization. While Classic Encryption utilizes AES-128-bit keys for field-level encryption, key management is fully automated with no Salesforce personnel participation in customer key creation. Customer keys are stored encrypted in the database using the Salesforce Master Application Key and remain encrypted except while in memory during encryption/decryption operations.
**Shield Platform Encryption (Additional Licensing Option)**
For organizations requiring AES 256-bit encryption at the application level, Salesforce offers Shield Platform Encryption as an additional subscription option. This solution provides:
- AES-256-bit keys for encrypting widely used standard fields, custom fields, and file attachments
- Hardware Security Module (HSM) based key management infrastructure
- Real-time key derivation with Digital Encryption Keys (DEK) that never persist in the database or file system
- Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) capabilities allowing customers to use open source crypto libraries, existing HSM infrastructure, or third-party key brokering services
- Complete customer control over key lifecycle management, including key-by-key opt-out options through UI and API
- FIPS 140-2 validated encryption libraries
**Data in Transit Encryption**
All transmissions between users and Salesforce Services are secured using TLS with International/Global SSL certificates utilizing 2048-bit Public Keys.
**Compliance Verification**

The encryption keys used as part of the Platform Encryption solution are unique to each customer organization. Question: The Contractor shall support encryption at the storage level.
AI Generated Answer:
## Storage-Level Encryption Capabilities
Salesforce provides comprehensive encryption at the storage level through multiple complementary approaches, meeting and exceeding standard requirements for data protection at rest.
**Foundational Storage Encryption**
All customer data stored within Salesforce services is encrypted at the storage level by default. According to our security documentation, customer data at rest is encrypted at the volume level using AWS Elastic Block Storage (EBS) with AES 256-bit encryption. This foundational layer ensures that all data stored within the Salesforce infrastructure maintains encryption protection without requiring customer configuration.
**Application-Level Encryption Options**
Salesforce offers two distinct encryption solutions that operate above the foundational storage encryption layer:
**Salesforce Classic Encryption** automatically creates a unique customer encryption key for each organization. The application manages key creation without personnel involvement, storing customer keys encrypted using the Salesforce Master Application Key. Customer keys remain encrypted except when temporarily in memory for data operations.
**Salesforce Platform Encryption** (available as an additional licensing option) provides advanced capabilities using Hardware Security Module (HSM) based key management infrastructure. The HSM and Digital Encryption Keys (DEK) are derived in real-time and never persist in the database or file system. This solution enables customers to use their existing HSM infrastructure, open source crypto libraries, or third-party key brokering services to manage tenant secrets outside Salesforce while maintaining the ability to revoke access at any time.
**Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) Capabilities**
Platform Encryption includes BYOK functionality, allowing customers to opt out of key derivation on a key-by-key basis through both UI and API interfaces. When customers opt out, they maintain full control over their keys and access to all key rotation features. The default service combines uploaded customer key material with Salesforce master secrets to derive unique data encryption keys, while the opt-out option allows customers to upload complete data encryption keys directly.
**MuleSoft Storage Encryption**
For MuleSoft services, customer metadata and organizational configurations are encrypted using AES-256 at rest. Customer payload data used in APIs and integrations remains transient within the MuleSoft environment and does not persist in storage.
**Compliance and Standards**
The encryption libraries for both Classic Encryption and Platform Encryption are FIPS 140-2 validated, meeting federal standards for cryptographic modules. Our HECVAT assessment data confirms affirmative compliance with storage-level encryption requirements across Salesforce platforms including Hyperforce, MuleSoft, and Tableau.
Salesforce fully supports encryption at the storage level through multiple layers of protection, from foundational volume-level encryption through advanced application-specific encryption with customer-controlled key management options.

The system shall identify fields that are in error.
The system shall allow an authorized user to define file types the system will allow or restrict.
The system shall maintain a consistent format and look across all reports (e.g. font/font size, headers, footers, page numbers, report run date, display of filters and sort options.)
The system shall allow authorized users to define access rights for ad-hoc reports.
The system shall allow authorized users to access the system from different platforms including tablets, phones, desktops and laptops.
The system shall provide context based help functionality.
The system shall allow authorized users to manage the display format of all data fields.
The system shall automatically adjust for different screen and device sizes.
The system shall display a consistent and uniform design across all screens using DIR's style guide.
The system shall display appropriate role based details for error messages.
The system shall allow authorized users to configure required fields.
The system shall provide keyboard navigation functionality.
The system shall allow authorized users to update displayed screen text without requiring programming.
The system shall allow authorized users to select individuals or groups to route documents and/or images to.
The system shall not require external partners to have vendor-specific software when using industry standard protocols.

The system shall allow for taking any individual infrastructure component of the system off-line, performing an upgrade or other maintenance activity, and placing that component back into production operation, without requiring system downtime during the process.
The system shall generate configurable error messages when data validation errors occur.
The system shall capture data in the designated system of record and avoid data duplication.
The system shall comply with State Administrative Manual (SAM) 5179: United States Postal Service, Optical Character Recognition Guidelines
The system shall comply with State Administrative Manual (SAM) 5180: United States Postal Service Zip+4 Guidelines
The system shall allow integration with document management systems document imaging, OCR, and electronic document management requirements.
The system shall generate a unique identifier for all users.

The system's infrastructure, including network resources, shall support projected growth during development and after implementation, and shall not impede future expansion of DIR's capabilities and services.
The system shall provide functions to view real-time and previous performance data for both application and infrastructure components.
The system shall provide report authoring environment for authorized users for creating summarized ad-hoc reports.
The system shall have the capability to execute reports on schedule and on-demand
The system shall provide the ability for authorized users to print reports.
The system shall allow authorized users to retrieve and view the archived reports.
Archived data shall be available for reporting.
The system shall provide planned and proven redundancy that includes automatic detection and failover mechanisms for each potential point of failure that could result in a service outage.

The system architecture and design shall utilize hardware or software to distribute connectivity and load processing across multiple, redundant computing resources for performance and failover capabilities.
The system shall automatically reconnect to resources, services and/or applications that were temporarily unavailable once the resources, services, and/or applications become available.
The system shall validate incoming data for data integrity violations.
The system shall restore archived data based on configurable parameters.
The system shall generate alerts and notifications based on configurable business rules.
The system shall process alerts to trigger an automated response based on configurable business rules.
The system shall make blank fillable and non-fillable e-forms available for the authorized users to download as per business rules.
The system shall automatically generate correspondence based on business rules
The system shall support various correspondence delivery methods (e.g., SMS, email, printed mail)
Archived data shall be available for viewing in real-time.
The system shall provide user interfaces that are customized at the field level, based on user roles.
The system shall allow users to save progress at each screen.
The system shall allow users to resume saved work in progress.
The system shall allow for keyword and full-text search for both structured content and unstructured content
The system shall provide confirmation before destructive or permanent actions are performed.
The system shall populate data into e-forms as specified in the business rules and requirements.
The system shall provide word processing capabilities for textual input (e.g. spell check, grammar, and thesaurus).
The system shall support English language or commonly used langauges for all system content and correspondence.
The system shall use consistent and standardized navigation layout and controls throughout the application.
The system shall use sequential navigation when a multi-step process must be addressed or completed (e.g., allow users to move to forwards or backwards previous pages).
The system search results shall be configurable to adjust the search results per page.
The system shall use search engine optimization techniques to provide most relevant search results to the users.
The system shall ensure data is stored securely and protected against unauthorized access and modifications
The system shall save passwords as one way encryptions which cannot be decrypted
The system shall ensure that no confidential information is displayed in tool-tip functionality (e.g., help text on specific fields)

The system shall allow authorized internal users (e.g., managers, EAMS Unit) to assign / change permission for internal users (e.g., individuals, user group, multiple user groups) and external users (e.g., including AFU), so that authorized internal users can easily and extensively update permissions in the system
They system shall allow the supervisors (e.g., presiding judges) to view and confirm the temporary permissions, so that supervisors can make any necessary changes to the permissions.

The system shall allow users to use keyboard shortcuts (e.g., press tab to move between fields, copy + X, paste with Ctrl + V, cut text with Ctrl + x) so that users can reduce the amount of time spent on entering information.
The system shall implement strong access control mechanisms to guarantee that privileged information associated with a petition is only accessible to authorized users
The system shall enable personalized settings and preferences for each user (e.g., personalization of screens, information shown, defaulting search filters to most recently used)

The system shall implement access control measures to ensure that confidential information from other departments is only accessible by authorized users, thereby maintaining data privacy and preventing unauthorized access
The system shall be capable of creating tasks or events in near real-time when documents are uploaded
The system shall have / enable accurate and reliable Optical Character Recognition (OCR) capabilities to ensure that all documents are correctly scanned and uploaded into the system
The system shall ensure that only authorized parties receive system generated notifications, to maintain the privacy and security of the information
The system shall enable showing data visualizations for analytics purposes that are user friendly (e.g., clear labels, legend, intuitive navigation)
The system shall provide a maximum response time, i.e., a timeout, of 10 mili seconds for search queries to ensure that users are notified if a search query fails or is not working as expected
The system shall be able to automatically link / attach documents filed in the system to a case, and the correct injured worker
The system shall ensure that only authorized parties (e.g., DEU, Medical Unit) have access to notes taken with sensitive data so that nobody who is unauthorized has access to sensitive information

The system shall automatically identify and suggest Pll redactions for manual approval when receiving or sharing information (e.g., documents filed, information viewed by self-service, data returned from public search).

Current Public search provides a great deal of information about Adjudication (ADJ) cases, but it will not include an injured worker's (IW) date of birth (DOB) or their address.Case events are only returned if events have taken place in the case. Not all case events are provided through this search. The events provided were determined through discussions with system users and were chosen because of their significance to the parties. Only case events that have taken place within the last six months are displayed, except for the filing of the application for adjudication of claim and the filing of a closing document.List of available information that is posted on the https://www.dir.ca.gov/dwc/EAMS/EAMS_PublicInformationSearch.htm

The system shall format communications in a standard and consistent way (e.g., no extra spaces, case # and date on the top of each page), so that users do not have to manually format documents in other systems (e.g., Word)
The system shall be able to store audio recordings, video, and 3-D data from trials / hearings
